name: PARCTAB Ubiquitous Computing Experiment
short_description: Palm-sized wireless handheld that used infrared room-cell location to adapt applications, control devices, and filter information to the user's physical position inside Xerox PARC — the first deployed context-aware computer.
long_description: Operational at Xerox PARC's Computer Science Lab from March 1993, the PARCTAB was a pen-based, palm-sized PDA that communicated via diffuse infrared to a network of in-building transceivers, each room forming a distinct location 'cell'. Because the system always knew which cell a tab occupied, it could filter files to nearby printers, adapt application menus to the user's position, forward calls to the nearest phone, control room lighting and temperature, and answer 'where is' queries for colleagues. The deployment grew from 25 tabs and 30 cells (March 1993) to 41 users and 50 cells (April 1994). Reported by Roy Want, Bill Schilit, Norman Adams, Rich Gold, Karin Petersen, David Goldberg, John Ellis, and Mark Weiser in IEEE Personal Communications (December 1995), it is recognized as the earliest fully deployed system to systematically demonstrate context-aware computing.
ai_note: Location sensing is purely rule-based infrared cell detection — no ML or AI; context-aware behaviour is deterministic rule dispatch based on room ID.

images.caption: Xerox PARCTab prototype at the Centre for Computing History, Cambridge. The palm-sized, pen-operated device communicated via infrared to room transceivers for location-aware computing.
images.alt_text: A palm-sized pen-operated computing device labelled as the Xerox PARCTab, shown on display at the Centre for Computing History museum.

sources.title: An overview of the PARCTAB ubiquitous computing experiment
sources.access_notes: R. Want, B. Schilit, N. Adams, R. Gold, K. Petersen, D. Goldberg, J. Ellis, M. Weiser. Vol. 2, No. 6, pp. 28–43, December 1995. Primary reference for deployment details, IR architecture, and context-aware application categories.

sources.title: The PARCTAB mobile computing system
sources.access_notes: B. Schilit, N. Adams, R. Gold, M. Tso, R. Want. The earliest paper describing the PARCTAB system architecture, October 1993.

sources.title: Context-aware computing applications
sources.publisher: IEEE Workshop on Mobile Computing Systems and Applications (WMCSA)
sources.access_notes: B. Schilit, N. Adams, R. Want. Defines four categories of context-aware applications (proximate selection, automatic contextual reconfiguration, contextual information/commands, content-triggered actions) using PARCTAB as the demonstration platform.

sources.title: Xerox PARCTab Prototype
sources.access_notes: Museum collection record for a physical PARCTab device. Confirms processor (Motorola), 4 MB RAM, pen interface, integrated microphone.

sources.title: Roy Want
sources.access_notes: Describes Want as principal designer and builder of the PARCTab while at Xerox PARC; credits the device as widely recognized as the world's first context-aware mobile computer.
